name: Universal Design for Learning (UDL)
description: Evaluates the degree to which the OER proactively removes barriers to learning through multiple means of representation, expression, and engagement.
preamble:
Universal Design for Learning (UDL) is a framework grounded in the neuroscience of learning. It asks us to design resources that are flexible enough to meet the needs of every learner from the outset — not as an accommodation but as a foundational design principle.

Reviewers should ask: could a student with dyslexia, ADHD, anxiety, limited prior knowledge, or a non-Western cultural background engage fully and successfully with this resource as designed?

criteria.title: Multiple Options for Representation & Perception
criteria.standard: Content is presented in multiple formats (text, audio, visual, interactive) to support diverse perceptual needs.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Content exists only in one modality (e.g., text only). No alternatives for learners who process differently.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Content is available in three or more modalities with seamless transitions between them.

criteria.title: Learner Choice in Expression & Demonstration
criteria.standard: Assessment options allow learners to demonstrate knowledge through varied means (written, oral, visual, etc.).
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: All assessment is in a single format with no alternatives offered.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Learners have genuine agency in selecting how they demonstrate mastery across a range of options.

criteria.title: Engagement, Relevance & Cultural Responsiveness
criteria.standard: Content connects to diverse learner backgrounds and contexts. Examples and scenarios reflect varied cultures and experiences.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Examples and scenarios center a narrow demographic. Cultural context is assumed or invisible.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Resource actively centers underrepresented perspectives and invites learners to bring their own context.

criteria.title: Transparent Expectations & Actionable Feedback
criteria.standard: Learning objectives are clearly stated. Feedback mechanisms are formative, specific, and actionable.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Objectives are vague or absent. Feedback is summative only, without guidance for improvement.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Objectives are co-constructed with learner input. Feedback loops are embedded and support self-regulation.

criteria.title: Collaboration, Communication & Community
criteria.standard: Opportunities for peer interaction and collaborative learning are intentionally designed into the resource.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Resource is entirely individualized with no collaborative learning opportunities.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Community of practice is embedded. Collaborative activities are structured, supported, and culturally responsive.

criteria.title: Metacognition, Self-Regulation & Strategic Learning
criteria.standard: Resource includes tools or prompts to support learner self-monitoring, goal setting, and reflection.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: No metacognitive scaffolding. Learners have no tools to monitor their own understanding.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Reflection and goal-setting are woven throughout. Learners are explicitly taught to use metacognitive strategies.

criteria.title: Equity, Bias Awareness & Inclusive Environments
criteria.standard: Resource is free from stereotypes and actively works to create a psychologically safe learning environment.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: Implicit or explicit biases are present. Language or examples exclude or stereotype learner groups.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Resource actively names and interrogates bias and privilege within the discipline and content.

criteria.title: OER-Specific UDL Considerations
criteria.standard: The open nature of the resource enables customization for diverse learner needs by instructors and institutions.
criteria.needsImprovementPrompt: OER format prevents meaningful adaptation for diverse contexts or learner populations.
criteria.exceedsPrompt: Resource is explicitly designed for remixing, with modular structure and an instructor guide for UDL-based customization.
